title: Non-overlapping landmark hazard stack instead of plain row classification
summary: Recurrent-event literature shows stacked landmark models can be biased when overlapping landmark periods are used.
rationale: Recurrent-event literature shows stacked landmark models can be biased when overlapping landmark periods are used. This task is exactly a repeated discrete-time next-event problem, so reframing rows as landmark hazards with non-overlapping evaluation windows is a cleaner inductive bias than ordinary iid classification.
implementation_hint: Create person-period style training with group keys at least (Year,Race,Driver,Stint). Train a binary hazard model for next-lap pit probability, but validate with race-chronology-safe, non-overlapping landmark folds and purge adjacent laps from train when the same driver-race appears in validation. Compare against current CV; if local score drops but fold variance tightens, trust the safer split.
expected_effect: May not spike raw CV immediately, but should reduce optimistic validation and improve transfer to hidden races/public LB.
risk: Main risk is lower local CV because the split is harsher; also easy to accidentally duplicate near-identical landmarks if stacking is not carefully purged.
